text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When team members review each other's code, they help catch bugs early before the code reaches production. This saves time and reduces the cost of fixing issues. Code reviews also promote knowledge sharing, as developers learn new techniques and solutions from their colleagues. Furthermore, they improve overall team quality by encouraging consistent coding standards and better design decisions. By reviewing code regularly, teams build a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement, leading to more reliable and maintainable software.
